things.name: Vigeland Sculpture Park
things.description: The world's largest sculpture park by a single artist — 212 bronze and granite figures by Gustav Vigeland

things.name: The National Museum
things.description: Norway's largest art museum housing Edvard Munch's The Scream and the country's finest collections

things.name: Akershus Fortress
things.description: Medieval castle and fortress on the Oslofjord — free to walk the grounds, with great harbour views

things.name: Fram Museum
things.description: The actual polar exploration ship Fram — the vessel that sailed further north and south than any other

things.name: Bygdoy Viking Ship Museum
things.description: Three magnificently preserved 9th-century Viking longships excavated from burial mounds

things.name: Oslo Opera House
things.description: Spectacular white Carrara marble building — walk up the sloping roof for views of the fjord and city

things.name: Holmenkollen Ski Jump
things.description: Oslo's iconic ski jump with a ski museum and panoramic view over the city and fjord

things.name: Aker Brygge & Tjuvholmen
things.description: Oslo's fashionable waterfront district with restaurants, galleries, the Astrup Fearnley Museum and marinas

things.name: Munch Museum
things.description: New 2021 tower museum holding the world's largest Edvard Munch collection with harbour views

things.name: Oslofjord Island Day Trip
things.description: Ferry hop between the idyllic islands of Bygdoy, Gressholmen and Hovedoya for swimming and picnics
